data.title: Thermo Fisher Rated Buy on Broad-Based Recovery, Conservative Guidance, and Attractive Valuation Versus Peers
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Larew has maintained their bullish stance on TMO stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Matt Larew has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that point to a durable recovery at Thermo Fisher. The company delivered a broad-based second-quarter outperformance across segments, customer types, and regions, and management raised full-year guidance more than the size of the beat while setting balanced expectations for the rest of the year, avoiding reliance on an aggressive late-year surge.

Previously weak end-markets such as biotech, academic/government, and China returned to growth and are backed by improving demand drivers, yet the company’s outlook only modestly embeds these tailwinds and instead leans on controllable levers like LPBS execution and recent integrations. Larew also highlights that Thermo Fisher’s valuation sits roughly in line with its long-term average and at a discount to life science tools peers, while the firm targets mid-single-digit organic growth and double-digit EPS expansion that he expects can be exceeded by 2027, supporting an Outperform recommendation.

Larew covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Thermo Fisher, Option Care Health, and Repligen. According to TipRanks, Larew has an average return of 10.0% and a 53.13% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, Bank of America Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $650.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Bread Financial: Strong Execution and Upgraded Outlook Drive Price Target Increase to $114, But Balanced Risk-Reward Keeps Hold Rating
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Moshe Orenbuch from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on Bread Financial Holdings, with a price target of $114.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Moshe Orenbuch has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, balancing strong recent execution with emerging signs of moderation. Bread Financial delivered earnings that exceeded expectations, supported by lower operating expenses, favorable reserve dynamics, and healthier fee-based revenues, and management also upgraded its outlook for loan and revenue expansion while improving its credit loss forecast.

At the same time, Orenbuch notes that some of the current profitability uplift is likely to be temporary, as partner-sharing arrangements are expected to absorb part of the recent RSA benefit later in the year, and early indications show that sales momentum slowed in July after a particularly robust June. As a result, even though he raised his EPS forecasts and increased his price target to $114, he believes the risk‑reward profile is balanced rather than clearly skewed to the upside, warranting a Hold recommendation.

In another report released on July 20, Morgan Stanley also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $102.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
